evals.prompt: Use R to remake the provided ecological heatmap plus taxonomy-flow figure in Nature style with simulated data. Assume R/Rscript is not installed locally.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must not use Python or any non-R plotting backend to draw a preview or export. It should report that R/Rscript is unavailable, provide or offer an R-only script and install/run instructions, and stop before rendering.

assertions.name: no_cross_backend_rendering
assertions.description: When R is selected and unavailable, no Python/matplotlib/seaborn/plotly preview, SVG, PDF, TIFF, or PNG is generated as a substitute.

assertions.name: selected_backend_blocker_reported
assertions.description: The response clearly reports the missing R runtime or package blocker and does not present a non-R figure as completed output.

evals.prompt: Use Python to make a Nature-style multi-panel heatmap and flow figure with simulated data. Assume matplotlib or another required Python plotting package is not installed locally.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must not use R or any non-Python plotting backend to draw a preview or export. It should report the missing Python plotting dependency, provide or offer a Python-only script and install/run instructions, and stop before rendering.

assertions.name: no_cross_backend_rendering
assertions.description: When Python is selected and unavailable, no R/ggplot2/ComplexHeatmap/patchwork preview, SVG, PDF, TIFF, or PNG is generated as a substitute.

assertions.name: selected_backend_blocker_reported
assertions.description: The response clearly reports the missing Python runtime or package blocker and does not present a non-Python figure as completed output.

evals.prompt: Make a Nature-style paper figure for my algorithm results. I want it to look polished and include a mechanism panel plus validation metrics.
evals.expected_output: The assistant asks the blocking question "Python or R?" and stops before generating mock data, scripts, previews, or exports because no backend was selected.

assertions.name: asks_python_or_r
assertions.description: The response asks the exact backend-selection question or an equivalent concise Python/R choice.

assertions.name: no_plotting_before_backend
assertions.description: The response does not create figure code, mock data, image files, or choose a backend by default.

evals.prompt: Use Python to draw a publication figure for an interception guidance algorithm. It should show the control loop, trajectory evidence, and comparison against baselines.
evals.expected_output: The assistant uses the Python-only workflow, states a claim-first figure contract, classifies the figure as engineering/control, loads or applies the engineering reference atlas, and plans a mechanism/control-loop hero panel with supporting trajectory and metric evidence rather than defaulting to a plain metric grid.

assertions.name: claim_first_contract
assertions.description: The response defines the core conclusion, reviewer question, panel evidence roles, and review risks before plotting.

assertions.name: engineering_reference_route
assertions.description: The response explicitly routes through the engineering/control reference atlas or uses its control-loop/trajectory visual patterns.

assertions.name: not_metric_grid_first
assertions.description: The first design is not just bars, lines, or a plain heatmap; it includes a mechanism or process hero panel.
